title: Quarter Zip Dude / Quarter Zip vs. Nike Tech / Quarter Zip and Matcha
about:
Quarter Zip Dude, Quarter Zip vs. Nike Tech and Quarter Zip and Matcha refer to a series of memes on TikTok about men who wear quarter zip sweaters, who are stereotypically wealthy, classy and hold white-collar jobs in finance. A TikTok trend about wearing quarter zips emerged in November 2025, when TikToker Jason Gyamfi (@whois.jason) shared a video about wearing a quarter zip and drinking matcha instead of wearing a Nike tech sweatsuit. The video followed the same format as the I'm a Matcha Nigga video and commented on Black male fashion and stereotypes, including the Performative Male stereotype.

In the following weeks, several memes surfaced on TikTok and other social media platforms that humorously contrasted quarter zips and Nike techs.
origin:
On November 3rd, 2025, TikToker[1] Jason Gyamfi (@whois.jason) shared a video in which he talked about being a "quarter zip dude." He said, "I don't do that Nike tech stuff, what y'all little boys do. I can't do that. I'm elegant. I'm classy." Over nine days, his video received over 903,100 likes.

On February 3rd, 2025, TikToker[2] @apnaj shared a video skit about a male character who asked for a Nike tech and instead was gifted a quarter zip sweater. The video's caption read, "Guys wear a quarter zip and change their whole personality." Over nine months, the video received over 748,100 likes.

Heading into 2025, other creators, like TikToker[3] @domg.o, shared viral videos about quarter zips with the same concept.
